page: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server featuring 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC Genoa cores running at 2.0 GHz. The system includes 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided via a 100 GB local NVMe SSD. The CPU architecture is x86_64 and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 (including AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16) and hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. No GPU is included in this configuration.

Benchmark results reveal a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance ranks in the top tier (top 10%), and single-threaded compression tasks like gzip and bzip3 decompression register strong performance in the top 25%. However, multi-core stress-ng performance drops to average, and the overall PassMark CPU score, physics test, and extended instructions test fall into the weak category (bottom 25%). Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is weak, though large block read and write bandwidths maintain average levels.

Due to the shared CPU allocation, this server exhibits performance bottlenecks under sustained multi-threaded workloads. Static web serving throughput is poor, placing in the bottom 10%, and database operations as well as Redis SET operations are weak. LLM inference on a 7B model is also poor at 5.0 tokens per second. Consequently, this server is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-light workloads, such as small-scale caching, single-threaded data processing, or development environments that benefit from a high memory-to-core ratio without requiring sustained, dedicated CPU performance.
description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 100 GB local NVMe SSD. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it supports AVX-512 and SHA_NI instruction sets. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression capabilities, though multi-core performance, database operations, and static web serving throughput fall into average to poor ranges. This resource density provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, offering qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, non-cpu-intensive workloads. It is suited for lightweight caching, single-threaded processing, and development environments.
og_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and 100 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-core but weak multi-core performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-m-2c-16gb-100s-amd is an AMD EPYC Genoa server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 100 GB NVMe SSD, suited for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A shared-core AMD EPYC Genoa server featuring a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs with 16.0 GB of system memory
100 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression
Weak multi-core, database, and static web serving benchmark results
Suited for memory-heavy, single-threaded processing and development tasks
